evals.name: feature-only-task-deletion
evals.prompt: I just added a delete-task feature to the kanban board â€” there's a trash icon on each task card that opens a confirm dialog, and after confirming the task disappears. Can you write a BDD feature file for this? The existing features live in tests/bdd/features/ and we already have steps for loading the board and seeing tasks in columns.
evals.expected_output: A new .feature file under tests/bdd/features/ that reuses the existing 'task board is loaded with seed data' background step, contains 2-3 scenarios (happy path delete, cancel from dialog, possibly delete-from-multiple-columns), reads declaratively (no CSS selectors in Gherkin), and has appropriate Feature/As-a-I-want-So-that header.

evals.name: generate-steps-for-feature
evals.prompt:
Here's a feature file I wrote for filtering tasks by date range. Can you generate the Playwright step definitions? The file is at tests/bdd/features/task-date-filter.feature with these scenarios:

Feature: Filter tasks by due date
  Background:
    Given the task board is loaded with seed data

  Scenario: Filter tasks due this week
    When I open the date filter
    And I select the "This week" preset
    Then I should only see tasks due this week

  Scenario: Filter tasks by custom date range
    When I open the date filter
    And I set the start date to "02/15/2026"
    And I set the end date to "02/25/2026"
    And I apply the filter
    Then every visible task should have a due date between "02/15/2026" and "02/25/2026"

Use the existing fixture file at tests/fixtures/api-mock.ts.
evals.expected_output: A tests/bdd/steps/task-date-filter.ts file that: imports test+expect from ../../fixtures/api-mock, uses createBdd(test) like the existing task-management.ts, implements each undefined step (not the background â€” that already exists), uses getByRole/getByLabel/getByText over CSS, uses await expect(...) web-first assertions, and handles the Material datepicker pattern with pressSequentially+Tab.

evals.name: scenario-outline-end-to-end
evals.prompt: Write a complete BDD test (feature + steps) for editing a task's priority. The user clicks a task card to open an edit dialog, changes the priority dropdown, clicks Save. Use a Scenario Outline so we cover changing from lowâ†’medium, lowâ†’high, mediumâ†’high, highâ†’low. Place files in tests/bdd/features/ and tests/bdd/steps/, and import from the existing api-mock fixtures.
evals.expected_output: Two files: a .feature with one Scenario Outline + Examples table covering 4 priority transitions (with parameterized scenario name for unique titles), and a steps .ts file that implements the open-edit/change-priority/save flow, scopes interactions to the mat-dialog-container, and asserts the priority badge updated. Step file uses createBdd(test) imported from the project's fixture module.

evals.name: tags-and-data-table
evals.prompt: We need a BDD scenario for bulk task creation. The user opens a 'Bulk add' dialog, pastes a list of titles (one per line), picks a priority for all of them, clicks Create All. Write the feature using a Gherkin data table for the titles, tag it @bulk and @slow because it triples the seed size, and generate the matching steps. Make sure the steps work with our route-mocked API in tests/fixtures/api-mock.ts.
evals.expected_output: Feature file with @bulk @slow tags, a scenario containing a Gherkin data table of titles (`| title |` rows), and step definitions that consume the DataTable parameter via .hashes() or similar. The 'create all' step iterates the rows. Step file uses the project's fixture import and Playwright web-first assertions. The @slow tag's implication (timeout bump) is either noted in the steps or handled via $test.setTimeout / a Before hook.
